summary: Tested ERP v3.0 Kuryos Beauty process-driven workflow end-to-end. Created pytest suite covering auth, admin reset, audit logs, blocking tasks, hierarchy enforcement, global sequential sample numbering (#101 start, /A /B variacoes), rework generating new global number (#102), retrabalho move being blocked, P&D pipeline gating with CQ task creation. Validated frontend: /tasks shows Bloqueante/Pendente badges, filters (Pendentes/Apenas bloqueantes/Concluídas/Todas), completion dialog with comment input, and /audit shows timeline with entity/action filters. 17/18 backend tests passed, 1 skipped (vendedor invite path not used). All critical flows working as specified.

minor.issue: PD_STATUSES does not include 'aprovado'/'completed'/'concluido' even though CQ task's blocks_stages targets them. This means the CQ task's blocks_stages currently can never trigger a 409 in PD pipeline because target close-stages don't exist. CQ task is correctly created with blocks_stages=[completed,aprovado,concluido,category=qa], but advancing to those names returns 400 'Status inválido' before blocking check. Consider either (a) adding these as valid PD_STATUSES or (b) renaming blocks_stages to match actual closure name in use (e.g., map sample stage on success).

issues: A11y warning: DialogContent missing Description or aria-describedby (React console warning)

action_items: (minor) Align PD_STATUSES or CQ task blocks_stages so CQ approval actually gates pd_card closure with 409 when admin tries to close the card.
(cosmetic) Add aria-describedby/Description to shadcn DialogContent in TasksPage complete dialog to silence a11y warning.
critical_code_review_comments: workflow_engine.assert_no_blocking_tasks returns HTTP 409 correctly for client/project but for sample retrabalho move the rule raises 400 from crm_routes.move_sample (correct, different semantics).
crm_routes.py is very large (>3000 lines); consider splitting into clients/projects/samples/pd modules for maintainability.
Audit log 'after' section for task_completed includes status + comment; consider also logging responsible_id for traceability.

Sample rework response shape is inconsistent with batch create response: rework returns {rework_sample, original_id, novo_numero} while batch returns {created:[...], count}. Consider standardizing to ease frontend/API consumer use.
PD card move endpoint uses field name 'status' while sample and client/project use 'stage'. Consider harmonising to reduce frontend complexity.

success_rate.backend: 100% (17/17 executed tests passed, 1 intentionally skipped because /api/users/invite flow not required for core ERP v3.0 validation)
success_rate.frontend: 100% (login, /tasks with filters + badges + complete dialog, /audit with filters + timeline — all rendered and integrated)

seed_data_creation: Tests reset tenant via POST /api/workflow/admin/reset-data and then create: 1 client (TEST_ERPv3 ClienteA in stage prospeccao->qualificado->projeto_em_discussao), 1 project (TEST_ProjetoA moved to amostras), 1 sample #101 with variations 101/A and 101/B plus 2 auto-created PD cards, 1 rework sample #102 preserving original.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test file /app/backend/tests/test_erp_v3_workflow.py covers the full ERP v3.0 process-driven flows and is idempotent (calls reset first). Auth is HttpOnly cookie-based (requests.Session works, no Authorization header needed). Payload gotchas: Client.contato_principal is a ContatoPrincipal object, not string; categoria_interesse is List[str]; Project batch uses field name 'projects' not 'projetos'; SampleBatchV2 uses 'samples' with 'nome_produto' + 'variacoes'; PD card move uses {'status': ...} not {'stage': ...}. Sample batch response is {'created': [...], 'count': N}. Rework response is {'rework_sample': {...}, 'original_id': ..., 'novo_numero': '102'}. PD_STATUSES currently = [solicitado, em_desenvolvimento, em_testes, aguardando_aprovacao, retrabalho_interno].
rca of the issue: No unresolved issues — one minor code inconsistency noted above about CQ task blocks_stages vs PD_STATUSES.
